HIGHER EDUCATION PROCUREMENT

Solicitation Title: Research Data Management and Discovery Platform
Procurement Reference: UNIV-2026-RESEARCH-015
Contracting Authority: Northern Universities Consortium (8 institutions)
Published Date: 20 March 2026
Closing Date: 20 May 2026 16:00
Estimated Contract Value: £1.9 Million
Contract Duration: 24 months (with optional 12-month extension)

PURPOSE

A consortium of eight UK research universities requires a unified research data management platform to support open research, enable data discovery, and facilitate collaboration across institutions while meeting UKRI funding council requirements for data management and sharing.

TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

Data Management Capabilities:
- Support datasets up to 100 GB per research project
- Storage for 5,000+ active research projects (100 TB aggregate capacity)
- Version control for research datasets with audit trail
- Metadata cataloguing with Dublin Core and DataCite standards
- Digital Object Identifier (DOI) minting for data citability

Discovery and Access:
- Federated search across all 8 university repositories
- Full-text search of metadata and datasets
- Advanced filtering (by funder, institution, research area, date)
- API for programmatic discovery and data access
- Export of metadata in standard formats (JSON, RDF, CSV)

Collaboration Features:
- Shared dataset access with fine-grained permissions (by user, group, institution)
- Data provenance tracking and researcher attribution
- Annotation and commenting capabilities for peer feedback
- Integration with Jupyter Notebooks for interactive analysis

Preservation & Compliance:
- FAIR data principles compliance (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable)
- 10-year data retention capability with digital preservation assurance
- Format migration planning for long-term sustainability
- Compliance with UKRI, Wellcome Trust, and EU Horizon grant requirements

EVALUATION CRITERIA

Technical Innovation and Feasibility: 35%
Alignment with Open Research Goals: 25%
Cost Efficiency and Scalability: 25%
Implementation Track Record: 15%

MANDATORY TECHNICAL SPECS

1. Handle 100 GB per-project average dataset size
2. Support 500+ concurrent users across 8 institutions
3. Metadata query response <2 seconds
4. Support 50+ metadata element types
5. Cloud deployment with disaster recovery (RTO 4 hours, RPO 1 hour)
6. OpenStack or commercial cloud infrastructure (AWS/Azure/GCP)
7. Integration with institutional single sign-on (Shibboleth/SAML)

OPTIONAL FEATURES

- Machine learning for dataset discovery and recommendations
- Advanced geospatial data visualisation
- Integration with computational workflow systems (Galaxy, CWL)
- Data analysis sandbox environment for secure exploration
- Blockchain audit trail for immutable data provenance

Submit to: research-data@northernconsortium.ac.uk
